questions.question: When Claude 'calls a tool', what is actually happening?
options: Claude directly executes code on the host machine
Claude emits a tool_use JSON block and the host app executes it
Claude sends an HTTP request to an external API
Claude writes code to a file and runs it
questions.explanation: Claude is a stateless text transformer — it never runs code directly. It emits a tool_use block (structured JSON), and the host application (e.g. Claude Code) actually executes the function and returns the result as tool_result.

questions.question: What does stop_reason: 'tool_use' tell the host application?
options: The conversation is over
Claude encountered an error
Execute the tool calls and send results back before showing output to the user
Claude is waiting for user input
questions.explanation: stop_reason: 'tool_use' means 'don't end the turn yet'. The host must execute the requested tools, return the results as tool_result messages, and call the API again. The turn only ends when stop_reason is 'end_turn'.

questions.question: A tool definition requires a description field to work — without it, the API call will fail.
options: True
False
questions.explanation: False — the API won't reject a missing description, but the model heavily relies on the description to decide when and how to use the tool. A missing or vague description leads to wrong or inconsistent tool usage. The description is the primary signal the model uses.

questions.question: You want to force Claude to use a specific tool called 'lookup_policy'. Which tool_choice config achieves this?
options: { "type": "auto" }
{ "type": "any" }
{ "type": "tool", "name": "lookup_policy" }
{ "type": "required", "name": "lookup_policy" }
questions.explanation: { "type": "tool", "name": "lookup_policy" } forces Claude to call that specific tool. 'auto' lets the model decide, 'any' forces at least one tool call but lets the model choose which.

questions.question:
This tool_result has a bug that will cause Claude to misinterpret a failure as success. Spot it:

```json
{
  "type": "tool_result",
  "tool_use_id": "abc123",
  "content": "Error: permission denied on /etc/shadow"
}
```
options: The tool_use_id format is wrong
The content should be an array, not a string
Missing is_error: true — Claude will treat the error message as successful output
The type field should be 'tool_response'
questions.explanation: Without is_error: true, Claude treats the content as a successful result. It might summarize 'Error: permission denied' as if it were real data. Always set is_error: true when the tool failed so Claude knows to reason about the failure.

questions.question: As a tech leader evaluating AI tooling: tools are best described as what?
options: Plugins that extend the AI model's internal capabilities
The mechanism by which an AI can request that the host app take actions on its behalf
Scripts that run automatically when certain keywords are detected
APIs that the AI directly calls over the network
questions.explanation: Tools are the bridge between the AI's 'thinking' (text generation) and real-world action. The AI requests, the host executes. This separation is fundamental — it means the AI can never take an action the host hasn't explicitly enabled and executed.
